title: LITMUS∞: SMT-Verified Memory Model Portability Checking

reviewers.name: Yuan Cheng
reviewers.key_strength: Wilson confidence interval methodology for cross-solver validation is statistically rigorous, and 100% certificate coverage with sub-millisecond solving enables practical CI deployment.
reviewers.key_weakness: Complete absence of probabilistic bug manifestation modeling means the tool treats all hazards as equally severe, limiting utility for risk-prioritized triage.

reviewers.name: Aniruddha Sinha
reviewers.key_strength: Dual-solver cross-validation with Alethe proof certificates and 140-pattern taxonomy surpasses most deployed verification tools in certification rigor and concurrency coverage.
reviewers.key_weakness: Proof certificates operate at litmus-test level, not program level—the gap between pattern-level safety and source-code guarantees is the entire concurrent program analysis problem.

reviewers.name: Lila Zhang
reviewers.key_strength: Clean SMT encoding with Alethe proof certificates and a well-designed declarative DSL (99.4% .cat correspondence) demonstrate strong symbolic reasoning foundations.
reviewers.key_weakness: DSL expressiveness relative to full .cat language is uncharacterized, and LLM fallback for code recognition is architecturally unsatisfying when richer symbolic methods would be superior.

reviewers.name: Sara Roy
reviewers.key_strength: Hybrid architecture correctly separates fallible ML recognition from trustworthy SMT verification, preserving formal soundness by construction even when the LLM errs.
reviewers.key_weakness: ML evaluation methodology falls below community standards: missing sample sizes, no model comparison, no calibration analysis, no confusion matrix, and undefined adversarial threat model.

reviewers.name: Joseph S. Chang
reviewers.key_strength: Proof-carrying architecture with Alethe certificates reduces the TCB to the formula generator and proof checker, achieving the gold standard for automated reasoning tool certification.
reviewers.key_weakness: No formal compositional proof calculus (frame rule) for multi-pattern programs—conservative shared-variable overapproximation lacks formal characterization.

consensus_strengths: Cross-solver validation (Z3 + CVC5) with 1,400/1,400 agreement provides exceptionally strong empirical evidence of encoding correctness across independent SMT implementations
Alethe proof certificates (993 UNSAT proofs + 407 SAT models) reduce the trusted computing base to auditable components, enabling truly independent verification
Sub-millisecond per-pattern solving with pip-installable CLI and JSON output enables practical CI/CD integration for formal memory model checking
140-pattern taxonomy with faithful memory model DSL (99.4% .cat correspondence) constitutes a reusable concurrency verification reference library
consensus_weaknesses: Proof certificates prove properties of litmus test encodings, not of user source code—the gap between pattern-level and program-level guarantees is unaddressed
LLM-assisted OOD evaluation (93.3%) lacks statistical rigor: unreported sample size, no confidence intervals, no model comparison, and undefined adversarial construction methodology
No compositional proof calculus for multi-pattern programs with shared variables—the conservative overapproximation lacks formal characterization as a frame rule or Galois connection
DSL expressiveness relative to full .cat language is uncharacterized—the single disagreement (1/171) is unresolved and may indicate deeper semantic limitations
AST analyzer's catastrophic failure on independent benchmarks (0% vs 96.6% on curated) reveals severe overfitting, and the LLM fallback treats the symptom rather than the cause
actionable_recommendations: Report full LLM evaluation statistics: sample size, 95% Wilson CIs, confusion matrix over pattern families, and comparison across at least three models including open-source alternatives
State and prove a formal frame rule for disjoint-variable composition, and formally characterize shared-variable overapproximation as an abstract interpretation with bounded imprecision
Resolve the single DSL/.cat disagreement definitively with published root-cause analysis, and formally characterize which .cat constructs (recursive relations, conditional ppo) the DSL cannot express
Develop a probabilistic bug manifestation model conditioning on pattern type, execution frequency, and architecture to enable risk-ranked output for triage
Invest in symbolic code normalization (abstract interpretation or term rewriting) to close the AST accuracy gap on out-of-distribution code rather than relying on LLM fallback
